text_original: A master of the art of war has said, 'I do not dare to be the host (to commence the war);
text_normalized: A master of the art of war has said, 'I do not dare to be the host (to commence the war);

text_original: I prefer to be the guest (to act on the defensive).
text_normalized: I prefer to be the guest (to act on the defensive).

text_original: I do not dare to advance an inch;
text_normalized: I do not dare to advance an inch;

text_original: I prefer to retire a foot.' This is called marshalling the ranks where there are no ranks;
text_normalized: I prefer to retire a foot.' This is called marshalling the ranks where there are no ranks;

text_original: baring the arms (to fight) where there are no arms to bare;
text_normalized: baring the arms (to fight) where there are no arms to bare;

text_original: grasping the weapon where there is no weapon to grasp;
text_normalized: grasping the weapon where there is no weapon to grasp;

text_original: advancing against the enemy where there is no enemy.
text_normalized: advancing against the enemy where there is no enemy.

text_original: There is no calamity greater than lightly engaging in war.
text_normalized: There is no calamity greater than lightly engaging in war.

text_original: To do that is near losing (the gentleness) which is so precious.
text_normalized: To do that is near losing (the gentleness) which is so precious.

text_original: Thus it is that when opposing weapons are (actually) crossed, he who deplores (the situation) conquers.
text_normalized: Thus it is that when opposing weapons are (actually) crossed, he who deplores (the situation) conquers.
